EN.title: Services inventory (Private)
EN.description: Single table of VaultWares + Prom‑King services: URL, where it runs, repo, and visibility.
EN.body:
# Services inventory (Private)

This page is a **single source of truth table** for “what we run”.

It exists so that:

- a non-technical person can understand *what each URL is*
- an assistant can quickly map a URL → machine → repo → deployment flow

> If you add a new public URL, update this table the same day.

Related:
- `operations/deployment-flow` (canonical deployment flow)
- `operations/health-ledger` (service health, Probe Joker, Alarm Joker, Token Joker)
- `operations/deploy-alerts` (deploy failure and bad-signature alerts)

## Table

| Service | URL | Where (runtime) | GitHub repo | Visibility |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Main website | `https://vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vaultwares-website/dist` (SPA) | `vaultwares-website` | Public |
| Docs | `https://docs.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vaultwares-docs/dist` (static) | `vaultwares-docs` | Tailnet-only |
| V.A.U.L.T Monitor | `https://monitor.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/monitor.vaultwares.ca` (SPA + `/monitor/*` proxy to VaultWares API over tailnet) | `vault-monitor` | Tailnet-only |
| Stats dashboard | `https://stats.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/stats.vaultwares.ca` (agent-ledger React/Vite SPA; `/monitor/*` and `/api/telemetry/*` proxy to VaultWares API over tailnet) | `agent-ledger` | Tailnet-only |
| API | `https://api.vaultwares.ca` / `http://100.67.25.118:9001` | DNS resolves to `vps-ovhcloud`; nginx owns `100.67.25.118:443` and proxies to FastAPI on `127.0.0.1:9001`; direct `:9001` remains the internal health/debug path. Tailscale Serve must stay disabled on OVH HTTPS `443`. | `vaultwares-api` (formerly `vaultwares-pipelines`) | Tailnet-only; app auth/gateway secret required |
| MCP server | `https://mcp.vaultwares.ca/mcp` | nginx HTTPS edge on `greencloud-vps` -> tailnet proxy to `vaultwares-mcp` on OVH (`vps-ovhcloud`) | `vaultwares-mcp` | Tailnet-only |
| vault-flows SPA | `https://flows.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vault-flows/dist` (SPA) | `vault-flows` | Public |
| noddit.org | `https://noddit.org` | `/var/www/vault-flows/dist` (SPA) | `vault-flows` | Public |
| GitHub webhooks | `https://hooks.vaultwares.ca/github` | nginx → `vw-webhookd` → `127.0.0.1:9033` | (ops service) | Public (required by GitHub) |
| Secrets manager | `https://warden.vaultwares.ca` | `vw-secretsd` → `127.0.0.1:9444` | (ops service) | Tailnet-only |
| (alias) secrets | `https://secrets.vaultwares.ca` | redirect → `warden.vaultwares.ca` | (ops config) | Tailnet-only |
| Prom‑King wallpaper site (PKT) | `https://prom-king.xyz` | Express API + React SPA running on port `4322` via systemd `pkt.service` at `/srv/repos/Prom-King/content-site` | `Prom-King/content-site` | Public |
| FullXXX prelanding/front door | `https://fullxxx.video` | WordPress front door at `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public`; FullXXX front-door variant is tracked separately | `Prom-King/prelanding-page` | Public |
| FullXXX tube site | `https://fullxxx.video` | WordPress plugin `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public/wp-content/plugins/fullxxx-video` | `Prom-King/tube-sites` | Public |
| Prom‑King link sharing | `https://links.prom-king.xyz` | Fastify on `127.0.0.1:3001` | `Prom-King/link-sharing` | Public |
| FullXXX link sharing | `https://links.fullxxx.video` | Fastify on `127.0.0.1:3001` | `Prom-King/link-sharing` | Public |


## Deployment and alert note

Every repo in this table must follow the same control-plane model:

- GitHub is only an event source (webhook)
- deploy/test/probe jobs happen inside the tailnet on the configured project
  target host
- **no GitHub Actions deploys**
- alert severity is determined by the Health Ledger alert profile, not by the
  host that receives the webhook
- local Ollama/Gemma remediation is allowed only for explicitly permitted
  non-production Low flows
- `vaultwares-api` owns stats/ledger telemetry DB access, batching ingest,
  auth, normalized monitor APIs, and future business rules. Static dashboards
  such as `stats.vaultwares.ca` must not read source ledger files or Postgres
  directly.
- VaultWares apps pass request correlation IDs as the `correlationId` query
  parameter, not as the primary header contract. VaultWares-generated IDs use
  `vw_{3-or-4-char Jira code}_c{7 hex chars}`, for example
  `vw_API_c123abc4`; non-VaultWares products may use their own prefix, for
  example `pk_PKT_c123456h`. The same correlation ID should persist from
  client request creation until the request completes or fails at the API
  boundary. Streams, downloads, and other continuous connections may create a
  child ID for the long-lived transfer.
- `vaultwares-api` request logging defaults to important events only: blocked
  requests, crashes, HTTP errors, and slow requests. Routine successful calls
  are intentionally suppressed to keep Kiwi volume usable. API syslog records
  must include source/client context in addition to the VPS proxy peer address.
- Vault-flows ComfyUI jobs submitted through `vaultwares-api` are serialized:
  one ComfyUI workflow job may execute at a time, regardless of source IP.
  Asynchronous queue clients can provide a completion callback URL; callbacks
  are limited to loopback, private, or tailnet hosts unless explicitly allowed.

Source of truth: `operations/deployment-flow`.

## Ops surfaces on `greencloud-vps` (not user-facing)

| Surface | Listener / unit | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `vw-webhookd` | systemd service, `127.0.0.1:9033` | Verifies GitHub webhook signatures + dispatches per-repo deploy scripts. Runs as `vwdeploy`. |
| `vaultwares-hooks` | systemd service, `127.0.0.1:8787` | Tailnet-only webhook handler (uvicorn). |
| `vw-secretsd` | systemd service, `127.0.0.1:9444` | Local secrets shim behind `warden.vaultwares.ca`. |
| `health-ledger-probe.service` | systemd service | Active Probe Joker loop under `/opt/health-ledger`; writes Health Ledger events and rollups. |
| `health-ledger-dashboard.service` | systemd service, `127.0.0.1:8790` | First-party Health Ledger dashboard; reads rollups/events and does not run probes. |
| `health-ledger-alarm.service` | systemd service, disabled until `/etc/health-ledger/alarm.env` is configured | Alarm Joker notification loop for incident locks and SMS/email/desktop fanout. |
| `vw-deny-watch.timer` | systemd timer, disabled 2026-06-03 | Legacy GitHub issue writer for webhook `bad_signature` clusters. Replaced by Health Ledger direction. |
| `vw-token-expiry-watch.timer` | systemd timer, disabled 2026-06-03 | Legacy GitHub issue writer for token expiry. Replaced by Token Joker direction. |

## Tailnet media surfaces on `vps-ovhcloud`

These services are intended for tailnet-only operator use. Docker-backed services
live on `vps-ovhcloud` in `/opt/vw-media-stack/docker-compose.yml` with
persistent config and data under `/srv/vw-media/`. Greencloud `dnsmasq` maps the
service hostnames below to `100.73.93.84`; Greencloud nginx then proxies to OVH
over the tailnet. Client DNS must include the matching Tailscale split-DNS/NRPT
names, otherwise public wildcard DNS resolves these hosts to Greencloud's public
address and the tailnet-only proxy path is bypassed.
Greencloud serves HTTPS for these hostnames with the
`/etc/letsencrypt/live/media.vaultwares.ca/` SAN certificate.

Greencloud also has explicit `listen 100.73.93.84:443 ssl;` entries for
tailnet service names such as `docs.vaultwares.ca`, `hooks.vaultwares.ca`,
`warden.vaultwares.ca`, `monitor.vaultwares.ca`, `stats.vaultwares.ca`, and
`mcp.vaultwares.ca`. Keep those listeners when adding private hostnames; without
them, nginx can select the media certificate on tailnet-originated requests.

Mullvad VPN routing for this stack is opt-in only. The staged gateway lives in
`/opt/vw-media-stack/docker-compose.mullvad.yml` as `mullvad-gateway`; no
service uses it unless an explicit override, such as
`docker-compose.mullvad.qbittorrent.yml`, moves that service into
`network_mode: service:mullvad-gateway`. Profile rotation is handled by
`/opt/vw-media-stack/bin/mullvad-rotate-profile` using dedicated VPS profiles
under `/opt/vw-media-stack/mullvad-profiles/`; personal-device address
`10.69.232.98/32` is intentionally rejected by default. The gateway keeps
`100.64.0.0/10` outside the tunnel so tailnet access, including
`100.67.25.118`, stays reachable. Do not add host-wide WireGuard routes for
this stack.

| Surface | Listener / unit | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `pyload-ng` | `https://pyload.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> `100.67.25.118:8003` -> container `8000` | pyLoad web UI and download manager. |
| `flaresolverr` | Docker, `100.67.25.118:8191` -> container `8191`; intended DNS: `flaresolverr.vaultwares.ca` | Cloudflare challenge solver API for compatible media/indexer tools. |
| `jackett` | `https://jackett.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> `100.67.25.118:9117` | Jackett indexer UI; installed natively under `/opt/Jackett`. |
| `qbittorrent` | `https://qbittorrent.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:8081` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:8081` -> container `8080` | qBittorrent web UI. |
| `sonarr` | `https://sonarr.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:8989` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:8989` | Series manager. |
| `radarr` | `https://radarr.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:7878` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:7878` | Movie manager. |
| `lidarr` | `https://lidarr.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:8686` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:8686` | Music manager. |
| `prowlarr` | `https://prowlarr.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:9696` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:9696` | Indexer manager. |
| `whisparr` | `https://whisparr.vaultwares.ca` -> Greencloud nginx -> OVH nginx `100.67.25.118:6969` -> Docker loopback `127.0.0.1:6969` | Media manager. |

## Residential egress proxy on `brume2`

Tube-site source CDNs (`fullvideos.xxx`, `1porn.tv`, `pornxp.*`) IP-block both
OVH and Greencloud datacenter ranges via Cloudflare and bind their signed MP4
URLs to the requester's IP. `shared-tube`'s `/api/stream/[slug]` route fetches
both the source page and the byte-range MP4 through a residential proxy on the
home-network Brume2 router so both legs egress the same residential IP.

| Surface | Listener / unit | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `tinyproxy` | `http://100.114.136.28:8888` (tailnet-only, allowlisted to `100.64.0.0/10`) | HTTP/CONNECT forward proxy on `brume2` (OpenWrt, GL-MT2500). Egresses the home WAN (`74.57.201.206`, Bell/Videotron). Read `operations/residential-egress-proxy` for the full architecture, env vars, and provisioning steps. |

Both `vps-ovhcloud` and `greencloud-vps` carry `STREAM_OUTBOUND_PROXY=http://100.114.136.28:8888` in `/etc/shared-tube/{fxv,pkt,oneporn,sexyprn}.env` so all four `shared-tube` apps share the same exit. The proxy is a single point of failure for video playback; see the troubleshooting section of the egress-proxy page if streams 502.

## Notes on the table above

- `fullxxx.video` is a **WordPress install** under `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public/`. Its front-door/prelanding behavior and tube plugins are tracked separately in Health Ledger so repo/runtime ownership is explicit. The plugins driving its catalog live under `wp-content/plugins/{fullxxx-video,tube-shared}` and are rsynced from `/var/www/tube-sites-src/` by the deploy script.
- `prom-king.xyz` runs a **standalone React/Express wallpaper application** at `/srv/repos/Prom-King/content-site`. Thumbs/meta are stored in `/srv/pkt-wallpapers`.
- The GitHub org **is `Prom-King`** (capital `P`, capital `K`, hyphen) for every repo in this list — the lowercase form `prom-king/...` won't match.

QC.title: Inventaire des services (Privé)
QC.description: Table unique des services VaultWares + Prom‑King: URL, où ça roule, repo, visibilité.
QC.body:
# Inventaire des services (Privé)

Cette page est une **table source de vérité** pour répondre à : “Qu’est-ce qu’on roule, pis où?”

Elle sert à :

- aider quelqu’un de non-tech à comprendre *c’est quoi chaque URL*
- aider un assistant à faire le lien **URL → machine → repo → déploiement**

> Si on ajoute une nouvelle URL publique, on met à jour cette table la même journée.

## Table

| Service | URL | Où (runtime) | Repo GitHub | Visibilité |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Site principal | `https://vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vaultwares-website/dist` (SPA) | `vaultwares-website` | Public |
| Docs | `https://docs.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vaultwares-docs/dist` (statique) | `vaultwares-docs` | Tailnet-only |
| V.A.U.L.T Monitor | `https://monitor.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/monitor.vaultwares.ca` (SPA + proxy `/monitor/*` vers l'API VaultWares via le tailnet) | `vault-monitor` | Tailnet-only |
| Stats dashboard | `https://stats.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/stats.vaultwares.ca` (SPA React/Vite d'agent-ledger; `/monitor/*` et `/api/telemetry/*` passent par l'API VaultWares via le tailnet) | `agent-ledger` | Tailnet-only |
| API | `https://api.vaultwares.ca` / `http://100.67.25.118:9001` | DNS vers `vps-ovhcloud`; nginx possède `100.67.25.118:443` et proxy vers FastAPI sur `127.0.0.1:9001`; le port direct `:9001` reste le chemin interne de santé/debug. Tailscale Serve doit rester désactivé sur HTTPS `443` sur OVH. | `vaultwares-api` | Tailnet-only; auth d'application / secret de gateway requis |
| Serveur MCP | `https://mcp.vaultwares.ca/mcp` | bord HTTPS nginx sur `greencloud-vps` -> proxy tailnet vers `vaultwares-mcp` sur OVH (`vps-ovhcloud`) | `vaultwares-mcp` | Tailnet-only |
| vault-flows SPA | `https://flows.vaultwares.ca` | `/var/www/vault-flows/dist` (SPA) | `vault-flows` | Public |
| noddit.org | `https://noddit.org` | `/var/www/vault-flows/dist` (SPA) | `vault-flows` | Public |
| Webhooks GitHub | `https://hooks.vaultwares.ca/github` | nginx → `vw-webhookd` → `127.0.0.1:9033` | (service ops) | Public (requis par GitHub) |
| Gestionnaire de secrets | `https://warden.vaultwares.ca` | `vw-secretsd` → `127.0.0.1:9444` | (service ops) | Tailnet-only |
| (alias) secrets | `https://secrets.vaultwares.ca` | redirection → `warden.vaultwares.ca` | (config ops) | Tailnet-only |
| Site de papiers peints Prom‑King (PKT) | `https://prom-king.xyz` | API Express + SPA React sur le port `4322` via systemd `pkt.service` dans `/srv/repos/Prom-King/content-site` | `Prom-King/content-site` | Public |
| Front door / prelanding FullXXX | `https://fullxxx.video` | front door WordPress à `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public`; la variante FullXXX est suivie séparément | `Prom-King/prelanding-page` | Public |
| Tube site FullXXX | `https://fullxxx.video` | plugin WordPress `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public/wp-content/plugins/fullxxx-video` | `Prom-King/tube-sites` | Public |
| Link sharing Prom‑King | `https://links.prom-king.xyz` | Fastify sur `127.0.0.1:3001` | `Prom-King/link-sharing` | Public |
| Link sharing FullXXX | `https://links.fullxxx.video` | Fastify sur `127.0.0.1:3001` | `Prom-King/link-sharing` | Public |


## Note de déploiement (pareil pour tout le monde)

Chaque repo dans cette table doit suivre **le même modèle de déploiement** :

- GitHub sert juste à envoyer l’événement (webhook)
- le déploiement se fait dans le tailnet sur `greencloud-vps`
- **aucun déploiement via GitHub Actions**

Source de vérité : `operations/deployment-flow`.

## Surfaces média tailnet sur `vps-ovhcloud`

Ces services sont destinés aux opérateurs dans le tailnet. Les services Docker
vivent sur `vps-ovhcloud` dans `/opt/vw-media-stack/docker-compose.yml`, avec
la config et les données persistantes sous `/srv/vw-media/`. `greencloud-vps`
répond aux noms privés via `dnsmasq`, puis nginx proxy vers OVH sur le tailnet.

Greencloud sert les surfaces média avec le certificat SAN
`/etc/letsencrypt/live/media.vaultwares.ca/`. Les autres noms privés
(`docs.vaultwares.ca`, `hooks.vaultwares.ca`, `warden.vaultwares.ca`,
`monitor.vaultwares.ca`, `stats.vaultwares.ca`, `mcp.vaultwares.ca`, etc.)
doivent garder des `listen 100.73.93.84:443 ssl;` explicites. Sans ces listeners,
nginx peut choisir le certificat média pour les requêtes qui arrivent par le
tailnet.

## Notes sur Prom-King / FullXXX

- `fullxxx.video` est une **installation WordPress** sous `/var/www/fullxxx.video/public/`. Sa page d'accueil/prelanding et ses plugins de tube sont suivis séparément dans le Health Ledger pour rendre l'ownership repo/runtime explicite. Les plugins qui alimentent son catalogue vivent sous `wp-content/plugins/{fullxxx-video,tube-shared}` et sont synchronisés via rsync depuis `/var/www/tube-sites-src/` par le script de déploiement.
- `prom-king.xyz` exécute une **application autonome de papiers peints React/Express** sous `/srv/repos/Prom-King/content-site`. Les miniatures et métadonnées sont stockées dans `/srv/pkt-wallpapers`.
- L'org GitHub est **`Prom-King`** (P et K majuscules, trait d'union) pour chaque repo de cette liste — la forme en minuscules `prom-king/...` ne correspondra pas.
